creator: raimeetravel
caption:
I’m not taking any chances with air travel this year! Especially with summer travel season coming up, I’m preparing even more because I just want as little stress as possible. #AMDpartner 

Other important things I do before a flight:

- Pack everything I want easy access to during the flight in my under the seat bag (eye patches, face mask, medications, snacks, headphones, @amd laptop, AirFly) 

- check my airline app for any last minute upgrades or better seats (this is how I’ve scored biz class for $400!) 

- hang out in the lounge and get free food if I have time! The credit cards I have usually grant me free lounge access!

Planning everything with my @amd always helps 👩🏼‍💻

transcription.text: This is my updated airport routine that makes the whole process of travel a lot less stressful. I personally think my method I use with my AMD laptop is the best way to avoid all of the travel chaos this year and feel more comfortable when you're traveling. First thing, starting 48 hours before my flight, I obsessively check the status because cancellations and delays are everywhere this year. I use my AMD AI powered laptop to plan and track everything. Next, I aim to get to the airport 1.5 hours before my flight, but I have pre-checked so security is faster. If you don't give yourself a little bit more time, then I always grab or bring an emotional support snack. It just calms me down, I don't know. I also make sure to download all of my movies and shows and podcasts. I don't trust plane wi-fi. Then, when it's time to board, I try to board early any way possible because I know I'll have overhead space. That alone lowers my stress so much. Then, when I'm in my seat, I go over my first day, I make any last minute changes, and make sure I know exactly what I'm doing when I land. And again, I love my AMD laptop for all of this. And then finally, I just do airplane mode, chill, watch movies, and I don't feel guilty for doing nothing. And I feel like flying is one of the only times you can feel that way. Okay, hope this helps your next trip. Safe travels. Bye!
